waldorf
Classic Waldorf salad recipe is defined by chopped raw celery with apple, walnuts, and grapes
gumbo
Built on the Cajun/Creole 'holy trinity' of onion, celery, and bell pepper
etouffee
Cajun sauce base is the onion-celery-bell pepper trinity
jambalaya
Uses the Cajun holy trinity (onion, celery, bell pepper) as its standard aromatic base
stuffing
Traditional bread stuffing/dressing recipes use diced celery as a core aromatic with onion
bolognese
Italian soffritto (onion, carrot, celery) is the traditional base of the ragu
minestrone
Mirepoix/soffritto base typically includes celery among the vegetables
osso buco
Braised with Italian soffritto (onion, carrot, sedano/celery)
pot pie
Chicken/turkey pot pie filling is standardly built on a mirepoix that includes celery
chicken noodle
Broth is classically simmered with a mirepoix (onion, carrot, celery)
consomme
Clarified stock is traditionally built on a mirepoix of onion, carrot, and celery
bloody mary
Classic recipe seasons the mix itself with celery salt, not just a garnish
bouillabaisse
French fish stew broth traditionally includes celery in the aromatic base
matzo ball
Chicken soup broth it's served in is commonly simmered with celery stalks
cioppino
Italian-American seafood stew base often starts with a celery-onion soffritto
gefilte
Poaching stock for gefilte fish is customarily made with celery, carrot, and onion
scotch broth
Scottish barley broth recipes commonly include celery among the root vegetables
